_notes: WNBA GAME DAY batch. Values verbatim from docs/wnba-gameday-venue-research.md (verified 2026-07-05). One key per venues/{doc id}. 10 docs: 2 standalone expansion arenas (coca-cola-coliseum, moda-center-portland-fire) + 8 shared NBA/NHL buildings. The `venue` field is a display annotation and is never written. Name corrections dropped in Phase 1 (footprint-center + entertainment-sports-arena already correct in Firestore). Mystics skipped (already 6/6). Two dead bag links replaced with confirmed-200 official pages: chase-center and climate-pledge-arena. gatesOpen omitted on crypto-com-arena (Kings) and climate-pledge-arena (Kraken) so the NHL co-tenant page keeps correct 'puck drop' wording; their WNBA pages use the 90-minute fallback. No em dashes.

coca-cola-coliseum.gatesOpen: Doors typically open 75 minutes before tip-off.
coca-cola-coliseum.parkingInfo: Exhibition Place operates roughly 5,000 shared parking spaces adjacent to the arena, commonly around $18, but they fill fast and tighten sharply when other Exhibition Place events run. Transit is the recommended arrival. Accessible parking is available through Exhibition Place.
coca-cola-coliseum.publicTransit: Transit is the easiest way in. Exhibition GO Station sits steps from the arena, one stop west of Union on the Lakeshore West line. TTC riders can take the 29 bus south to Exhibition or the 509/511 streetcar corridor. PRESTO offers up to 15 percent off select home games with code PRESTOTEMPO.
coca-cola-coliseum.accessibility: Barrier-free accessible seating is sold through Ticketmaster, with sections marked by an "A." Contact Fan Services in advance for specific accommodations.
coca-cola-coliseum.nearby: Liberty Village, an easy walk north, is the closest food and drink district (Local Public Eatery, Brazen Head, and more). Hotel X Toronto sits inside Exhibition Place for a walk-to-court stay. The venue is cashless with no re-entry.

moda-center-portland-fire.gatesOpen: Doors typically open 90 minutes before tip-off.
moda-center-portland-fire.parkingInfo: Four Rose Quarter garages (the Garden garage is attached to the arena, plus East and West Broadway and the Annex) and the Benton surface lot offer 2,500-plus spaces, roughly $18 to $35 on event nights, bookable through Ticketmaster. The U-Store Rose Quarter lot is the cheaper walkable option at about $5 to $8. The off-site NE 7th lots are Blazers-only.
moda-center-portland-fire.publicTransit: MAX Light Rail is the easiest option: the Blue, Green, Red, and Yellow lines all stop at the Rose Quarter Transit Center directly next to the arena, with extra trains on event nights. TriMet buses (4, 8, 35, 44, 77, 85) also serve the transit center. Pay by contactless tap or Hop Fastpass; an adult day pass runs about $5.60.
moda-center-portland-fire.accessibility: The arena is KultureCity Sensory Inclusive certified and offers OneCourt haptic devices for blind and low-vision fans, with accessible parking in every garage.
moda-center-portland-fire.nearby: The Lloyd District has pre and post-game food plus free weeknight street parking after 7pm; Spirit of '77 and the game-day Dr. Jack's brewpub are close by. Hotel Eastlund is about a 5-minute walk. The campus is cashless and EV charging is complimentary with paid parking.

chase-center.gatesOpen: Doors typically open 90 minutes before tip-off.
chase-center.parkingInfo: There is no free arena lot. The Mercedes-Benz Garage prepaid pass via Ticketmaster is premium-priced; nearby UCSF Mission Bay lots run about $20 to $30 a short walk away. Mission Bay street parking is permit-only and aggressively enforced, so prepay or take transit.
chase-center.publicTransit: The Muni Metro T Third line stops at UCSF/Chase Center right at the south entrance, and your event ticket doubles as a free all-day Muni pass. Connect from BART at Embarcadero or Montgomery to the T Third, or from Caltrain at 4th and King (about a 12-minute walk). Ferry service to Pier 48 1/2 runs on some game days.
chase-center.accessibility: Accessible parking, seating, hearing assistance, and service-animal details are covered in the arena's A-to-Z Guide; contact guest experiences for specifics.
chase-center.nearby: The surrounding Thrive City has restaurants and bars open on event days, and Oracle Park is a 12 to 15-minute walk for double-headers. Bags are limited to 14x6x14 with no backpacks, and a bag valet operates on 16th Street.

gainbridge-fieldhouse.gatesOpen: Doors typically open 60 minutes before tip-off.
gainbridge-fieldhouse.parkingInfo: The Virginia Avenue Garage connects to the arena by a third-floor sky bridge (about 2,200 spaces) and is the closest option; book via ParkWhiz, the venue's recommended platform. Nearby CityWay North (~$12) and lots on Alabama and Pearl (~$10) are cheaper. Downtown meters are free on Sundays.
gainbridge-fieldhouse.publicTransit: IndyGo buses serve the area, with the Julia M. Carson Transit Center about two blocks away and the IndyGo Red Line running through downtown; the two-hour fare is $1.75. Amtrak's Cardinal line stops at Union Station, roughly a 10-minute walk.
gainbridge-fieldhouse.accessibility: Wheelchair escorts are available on request at entrances, elevators and escalators connect all levels via the pedestrian bridge, and gender-neutral and family restrooms plus mothers' rooms are on multiple levels.
gainbridge-fieldhouse.nearby: The Fieldhouse sits in the heart of downtown Indy with restaurants and bars within walking distance. It is cashless, and bags are limited to 6x10x2 with no backpacks or hard-sided bags.

crypto-com-arena.venue: Crypto.com Arena (los-angeles-sparks, shared with Kings [NHL]); gatesOpen intentionally omitted (NHL co-tenant), WNBA page uses 90-min fallback
crypto-com-arena.parkingInfo: The arena's own lots hold about 3,300 spaces at roughly $30 to $50; the West Garage (Lot W) offers a flat event rate and stay-as-late-as-you-want convenience. More than 10,000 additional spaces sit within a 7 to 10-minute walk. Prepay via SpotHero or ParkWhiz for better rates; no in-and-out privileges.
crypto-com-arena.publicTransit: The Metro A and E lines stop at Pico Station, a 1 to 2-block walk to the arena; from downtown transfer at 7th St/Metro Center. DASH Route F and several Metro bus lines also stop near L.A. LIVE. Load your TAP card before the event to skip post-game lines.
crypto-com-arena.accessibility: Designated accessible and van-accessible parking is available in the garages for vehicles with a valid placard, on a first-come basis; see the arena parking map and A-Z Guide.
crypto-com-arena.nearby: L.A. LIVE next door has restaurants, bars, and the Microsoft Theater. The bag policy is strict: clutches and wallets under 5x9x1 only, no backpacks, purses, clear bags, or fanny packs; Binbox lockers by the Kobe Bryant Entrance hold larger items. Cashless venue.

target-center.gatesOpen: Doors typically open 90 minutes before tip-off.
target-center.parkingInfo: Nearly 25,000 spaces sit across roughly 38 downtown ramps and lots, most a short walk or skyway connection away; Ramps A, B, and C are closest. Book via SpotHero for rates up to 50 percent off drive-up. On-street metered parking is limited.
target-center.publicTransit: The METRO Blue and Green light rail lines both stop at Warehouse District/Hennepin Avenue Station, a block south of the arena on 1st Avenue; numerous Metro Transit bus routes also serve downtown. In winter, the downtown skyway system connects many ramps to the arena indoors.
target-center.accessibility: Guests needing an elevator should park in Ramp A or B and enter at skyway level; the ADA drop-off is at 1st Avenue and 6th Street by the Life Time Lobby.
target-center.nearby: The arena sits in the Warehouse and Entertainment District with restaurants and bars steps away; the Loews, Marriott City Center, and Hampton Inn are all within a couple of blocks. Bags must be no larger than 5x9, and bag check is available for a fee.

barclays-center.gatesOpen: Doors typically open 90 minutes before tip-off.
barclays-center.parkingInfo: There is no arena-owned lot. Nearby garages on Dean and Pacific Streets run roughly $30 to $60; SpotHero is an official partner for prepaid spots. Public transit is strongly recommended over driving.
barclays-center.publicTransit: The arena sits directly above the Atlantic Avenue-Barclays Center station, served by the 2, 3, 4, 5, B, D, N, Q, and R trains (with C and G nearby), plus LIRR into Atlantic Terminal. From Manhattan the 2/3 express or D/N/Q reaches it in about 15 minutes with no transfer.
barclays-center.accessibility: Accessible seating with companion seats is on every level, elevators serve Sections 1, 11, and 20, and Guest Services at the main entrance assists with wheelchair access and assistive listening.
barclays-center.nearby: Prospect Heights, Fort Greene, and Downtown Brooklyn surround the arena with dining and nightlife. Bags larger than 10x6x2 and hard-sided bags are not permitted; small clutches are allowed.

footprint-center.venue: Mortgage Matchup Center (phoenix-mercury, shared with Suns; name already correct in Firestore)
footprint-center.gatesOpen: Doors typically open 90 minutes before tip-off.
footprint-center.parkingInfo: Several downtown Phoenix garages and lots sit within walking distance; prepay through ParkWhiz for the easiest arrival. Event parking generally opens about 30 minutes before doors. Rates and availability vary by event.
footprint-center.publicTransit: Valley Metro light rail runs right past the arena, with the closest stops at 3rd Street/Jefferson and 3rd Street/Washington. Through the RailRide program, any valid arena or Ticketmaster event ticket is free light rail fare on event day, from four hours before through the end of service.
footprint-center.accessibility: The arena is fully ADA-compliant; contact the box office or venue for accessible seating and accommodations.
footprint-center.nearby: CityScape and the Legends Entertainment District sit steps away for pre and post-game food and drinks, with Roosevelt Row's murals and breweries a short trip north. Bags over 14x14x6 and backpacks are prohibited; clutches under 4x6 use the express entry lanes.

climate-pledge-arena.venue: Climate Pledge Arena (seattle-storm, shared with Kraken [NHL]); gatesOpen intentionally omitted (NHL co-tenant), WNBA page uses 90-min fallback
climate-pledge-arena.parkingInfo: Three garages serve the arena: the Arena Garage (accessed by underground tunnel, complimentary EV charging), the 1st Ave N Garage, and the Seattle Center 5th Ave N Garage, commonly $20 to $55; prepurchase through the Kraken + CPA app. The closest ADA parking is in the Arena and 1st Ave N garages.
climate-pledge-arena.publicTransit: Every event ticket includes a free King County transit pass valid two hours before and after. The Seattle Center Monorail is a 90-second ride from Westlake downtown; Link light rail riders transfer to the Monorail at Westlake, and King County Metro buses serve the Seattle Center campus.
climate-pledge-arena.accessibility: ADA parking sits in the Arena and 1st Ave N garages, and the arena is designed to exceed accessibility standards; see the venue guide for seating and services.
climate-pledge-arena.nearby: The arena anchors the 74-acre Seattle Center campus at the base of the Space Needle, with Uptown and Belltown dining a short walk away. Small clutches under about 4x6x1.5 enter any door; larger bags go through x-ray at designated entries.
